items.title: Getting Started
items.text:
A multi-agent development system where you describe what you want to build in plain language, and eight specialized AI agents plan, develop, test, secure, and ship it — orchestrated in real time throu

This guide covers the prerequisites, installation, and project structure for agent-orchestra.

items.title: Prerequisites
items.text:
• TypeScript development environment
  • Python development environment
  • Markdown development environment
  • JSON development environment
  • Shell development environment
  • YAML development environment
  • Dockerfile development environment
  • JavaScript development environment

items.title: Installation
items.text:
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/morbidsteve/agent-orchestra
cd agent-orchestra
npmyarnpnpm 
npm install

yarn install

pnpm install

items.title: Troubleshooting Guide for Agent-Orchestra
items.text: Don't worry if you're experiencing issues with Agent-Orchestra - we're here to help! This guide will walk you through common problems and solutions to get you back up and running.

items.title: General Troubleshooting Steps
items.text: Before diving into specific error types, let's cover some general troubleshooting steps:

items.title: Installation
items.text:
• Make sure you've installed Agent-Orchestra correctly by following the official installation instructions.
  • Verify that you've installed the required dependencies and that they're up-to-date.

items.title: Configuration
items.text:
• Double-check your configuration files for any syntax errors or typos.
  • Ensure that you've set up the necessary environment variables.

items.title: Runtime
items.text:
• Check the console output for any error messages or warnings.
  • Try running Agent-Orchestra with the --verbose flag to get more detailed output.

items.title: No Error Messages
items.text:
If you're not seeing any error messages, but Agent-Orchestra isn't working as expected:

  • Try running Agent-Orchestra with the --debug flag to enable debug mode.
  • Check the Agent-Orchestra logs for any clues.

items.title: Unexpected Behavior
items.text:
If Agent-Orchestra is behaving unexpectedly:

  • Check the documentation to ensure you're using the correct commands and options.
  • Try resetting Agent-Orchestra to its default settings.

items.title: Getting Help
items.text:
If you're still having trouble, don't worry! There are a few ways to get help:

  • Community Forum: Head over to our community forum to ask questions and get help from other users and developers.
  • Issue Tracker: If you suspect a bug, create an issue on our issue tracker with as much detail as possible.
  • Documentation: Double-check our documentation to ensure you haven't missed anything.

items.title: Additional Tips
items.text:
• Make sure you're running the latest version of Agent-Orchestra.
  • Keep an eye on the Agent-Orchestra repository for updates and releases.

items.title: Getting Started with Agent-Orchestra
items.text: Welcome to Agent-Orchestra! We're excited to help you get started with this powerful tool. In this tutorial, we'll walk you through the installation process and show you a practical "first use" walkthrough.

items.title: Prerequisites
items.text:
Before we begin, make sure you have the following:

  • A computer with a compatible operating system (Windows, macOS, or Linux)
  • A code editor or IDE (Integrated Development Environment) of your choice

items.title: Step 1: Installation
items.text:
Unfortunately, we couldn't detect the package manager used by Agent-Orchestra. However, we can provide general installation instructions. Please consult the official documentation or community resources for specific installation instructions on your platform.

That being said, here are some general steps you can follow:

  • Using a package manager: If you're using a package manager like Homebrew (on macOS) or apt-get (on Linux), you can try running: 
    # Example command (replace with actual package manager command)
brew install agent-orchestra
  • From source: If you're installing from source, you'll need to clone the repository and follow the build instructions: 
    git clone https://github.com/agent-orchestra/agent-orchestra.git
cd agent-orchestra
# Follow build instructions in README.md

items.title: Step 2: Verify Installation
items.text:
Once you've installed Agent-Orchestra, verify that it's working correctly by running: 

# Example command (replace with actual verification command)
agent-orchestra --version
This should display the version number of Agent-Orchestra.

items.title: Step 3: First Use Walkthrough
items.text: Let's create a simple example to get you started with Agent-Orchestra.

items.title: Create a New Project
items.text:
Create a new directory for your project and navigate to it: 

mkdir my-agent-orchestra-project
cd my-agent-orchestra-project

items.title: Initialize the Project
items.text:
Initialize the project with Agent-Orchestra: 

# Example command (replace with actual initialization command)
agent-orchestra init
Follow the prompts to set up your project.

items.title: Run the Example
items.text:
Run the example code to see Agent-Orchestra in action: 

# Example command (replace with actual run command)
agent-orchestra run example
This should output a success message.

items.title: Conclusion
items.text:
Congratulations! You've successfully installed and used Agent-Orchestra for the first time. We hope this tutorial has been helpful in getting you started with this powerful tool.

If you encounter any issues or have questions, don't hesitate to reach out to the community or consult the official documentation.

Happy orchestrating!

items.title: What is Agent Orchestra?
items.text: Agent Orchestra is a powerful tool that helps you manage and visualize complex data from multiple sources. It's designed to make it easy to understand and work with data from different agents, making it a great solution for teams and organizations that need to make sense of large amounts of information.

items.title: Who is it for?
items.text:
Agent Orchestra is perfect for teams and organizations that need to collect, analyze, and visualize data from multiple sources. This might include:

  • Data analysts and scientists who need to work with large datasets
  • IT teams who need to monitor and troubleshoot complex systems
  • Business users who want to gain insights into their operations and make data-driven decisions

items.title: Main Capabilities
items.text:
Here are some of the key features that make Agent Orchestra so useful:

  • Data Visualization: Agent Orchestra provides a range of visualization tools, including charts, graphs, and timelines, to help you understand complex data.
  • Multi-Source Support: Agent Orchestra can collect data from multiple sources, including APIs, databases, and files.
  • Real-time Updates: Agent Orchestra provides real-time updates, so you can see changes as they happen.
  • Collaboration: Agent Orchestra makes it easy to share data and insights with team members, so you can work together to make sense of your data.

items.title: Getting Started
items.text: Getting started with Agent Orchestra is easy. Our documentation will walk you through the process of setting up and configuring Agent Orchestra for your needs. We'll also provide you with tips and best practices for getting the most out of the tool.

items.title: orchestrator.py
items.text:
Multi-Agent Orchestrator

The master agent that coordinates developer, tester, devsecops, and business-dev agents. Receives high-level tasks and delegates to specialists based on the work needed.

Usage: # Feature development (full pipeline) python orchestrator.py "Build a user authentication module with OAuth2 support"

# Specific workflow
python orchestrator.py --workflow=security-audit

# Business analysis
python orchestrator.py --workflow=feature-eval "Add real-time collaboration"

# Code review on a PR
python orchestrator.py --workflow=code-review --target=feature/auth-module
Relevant source files The following files were used as context for this page: - [`orchestrator.py`](https://github.com/morbidsteve/agent-orchestra/blob/main/orchestrator.py) — this module (371 lines) Source orchestrator.py Language Python Lines 371 Exports 5

items.title: mcp_bridge_dynamic.py
items.text:
Dynamic MCP Bridge — Exposes spawn_agent, get_agent_status, and ask_user tools to the orchestrator Claude CLI session via MCP stdio protocol.

Environment variables (set by dynamic_orchestrator.py before launching): ORCHESTRA_API_URL — Backend HTTP URL (default: http://127.0.0.1:8000) ORCHESTRA_EXECUTION_ID — Current execution ID ORCHESTRA_INTERNAL_TOKEN — Shared auth token

Generate OAuth PKCE params and return the authorization URL.

Generate OAuth PKCE params and return the authorization URL.

This implements the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE flow directly,
bypassing the ``claude auth login`` CLI.  The redirect goes to
Anthropic's server (not localhost), so no port-forwarding is required
inside Docker.

AI Summary

This module wraps agent commands in docker run for bare-metal execution. It ensures the agent Docker image exists, building it if necessary, and broadcasts build progress via WebSocket. The module provides a way to run agent commands in a Docker environment.

items.title: detect_sandbox
items.text:
def detect_sandbox() -> SandboxStatus

View source

Detect whether the process is running inside a container.

Detect whether the process is running inside a container.

Checks (in order):
1. DEVCONTAINER env var (VS Code devcontainer)
2. ORCHESTRA_CONTAINER env var (explicit opt-in)
3. /.dockerenv file (Docker)
4. /proc/1/cgroup contents (cgroup-based detection)
5. BACKEND_HOST=0.0.0.0 (common container binding)

Also checks Docker availability for bare-metal auto-containerization.

Returns: SandboxStatus

items.title: orchestrator.py
items.text:
Multi-Agent Orchestrator

The master agent that coordinates developer, tester, devsecops, and business-dev agents. Receives high-level tasks and delegates to specialists based on the work needed.

Usage: # Feature development (full pipeline) python orchestrator.py "Build a user authentication module with OAuth2 support"

# Specific workflow
python orchestrator.py --workflow=security-audit

# Business analysis
python orchestrator.py --workflow=feature-eval "Add real-time collaboration"

# Code review on a PR
python orchestrator.py --workflow=code-review --target=feature/auth-module
Relevant source files The following files were used as context for this page: - [`orchestra-dashboard/orchestrator/orchestrator.py`](https://github.com/morbidsteve/agent-orchestra/blob/main/orchestra-dashboard/orchestrator/orchestrator.py) — this module (368 lines) Source orchestra-dashboard/orchestrator/orchestrator.py Language Python Lines 368 Exports 5

items.title: Exports
items.text:
Exports (1)Internal (7) Name Kind Description useOfficeState function Derives office visualization state from WebSocket messages for a given execution.
  • PHASE_AGENTS — constant — Maps backend pipeline phase names to agent roles for office visualization.
  • ROLE_COLORS — constant — Color lookup for known agent roles.
  • ROLE_ICONS — constant — Icon lookup for known agent roles.
  • hashColor — function — Generate a stable HSL color from any role string.
  • roleToName — function — Derive a human-readable name from a role slug (e.g. "business-dev" -> "Business Dev").
  • createAgentNode — function — Create an AgentNode dynamically for a given role.
  • dynamicAgentToNode — function — Convert a DynamicAgent from a spawn message into an AgentNode for office visualization.

items.title: useOfficeState
items.text:
function useOfficeState(executionId: string | null): OfficeState

View source

Derives office visualization state from WebSocket messages for a given execution.

Derives office visualization state from WebSocket messages for a given execution. Initializes agents in idle state and updates based on agent-status and agent-connection messages. Supports dynamic agents via agent-spawn/agent-output/agent-complete messages, while maintaining backward compatibility with legacy agent-status messages.

Parameters:

Name Type Default Description executionId string | null

Returns: OfficeState

function calculateAgentPositions(count: number): AgentPosition[]

View source

Calculate positions for N agents in concentric rings around center (50, 50).

Calculate positions for N agents in concentric rings around center (50, 50). Ring 1 (radius 28%): up to 6 agents Ring 2 (radius 42%): up to 10 agents Positions are stable — adding a new agent doesn't shift existing ones.
